rationale.R1.2: A review of the section centrally includes checking whether the evaluation protocol, aggregation, agreement reporting, and interpretation cohere logically.
rationale.R1.3: The section reports ratings and agreement values, so review responsibility includes checking whether interpretive claims follow from the reported results.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation most directly asks for section review, and the artifact contains prominent sentence-level grammar, tense, flow, and concision issues.
rationale.R1.7: The section invokes prior studies and dataset/report provenance, so review includes checking attribution adequacy and scholarship signals.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer should disclose uncertainty where the artifact lacks details such as exact study references, statistical tests, or calibration criteria.
rationale.RX.2: The review should avoid endorsing claims stronger than the reported human-evaluation evidence supports.
rationale.RX.3: The response must stay within reviewing the provided section rather than rewriting or expanding the paper.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns clinical radiology reports, review responsibility includes avoiding advice that could amplify unsupported clinical claims.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected review responsibility should remain traceable to the provided section text and its reported numbers.
